page: Vultr vhf-1c-2gb is an entry-level virtual machine belonging to the High Frequency Compute family. It features a single shared vCPU based on the x86_64 architecture, utilizing an Intel Core Processor (Skylake, IBRS) clocked at 2.0 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 2.0 GB of system memory and 64 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. It does not include dedicated GPU hardware.

Benchmark results indicate modest processing capabilities. The single-core and multi-core stress-ng CPU tests both scored 1266.88, placing the instance in the lower performance tiers. Memory bandwidth is limited, with read-only cached small block speeds at 100.1 GB/sec and uncached large block read/write speeds measuring 11.09 GB/sec and 10.16 GB/sec respectively. However, single-threaded gzip compression demonstrates average performance at 24.19 GB/sec, while single-threaded bzip3 decompression performs at 8.48 GB/sec.

Given the shared CPU allocation and compact resource footprint, this instance offers a cost-effective entry point for lightweight workloads. The inclusion of 64 GB NVMe SSD storage provides fast local I/O relative to its resource class. This server is suited for low-traffic web serving, basic utility scripts, lightweight development environments, and simple background tasks that do not require sustained high CPU utilization.
description: Vultr vhf-1c-2gb is an entry-level virtual server in the High Frequency Compute family, featuring a single shared x86_64 Intel Skylake vCPU clocked at 2.0 GHz, 2.0 GB of RAM, and 64 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Lacking a dedicated GPU, this instance is designed for basic computing needs. Benchmark data shows modest performance, with a stress-ng CPU score of 1266.88 and uncached memory read bandwidth of 11.09 GB/sec, though it achieves an average single-threaded gzip compression rate of 24.19 GB/sec. The shared-core architecture makes it a highly cost-efficient option for workloads with low resource demands. It is best suited for lightweight applications, development environments, simple automation scripts, and low-traffic web hosting.
